Ride motion tuner

The homepage flight is one short generated clip. Scrubbed raw across a long track it reads as a still image, which is why the ride felt broken. Motion is now amplified in drawFrame: a scroll-driven push-in plus a diagonal drift, with the copy travelling against the world for real parallax. Drag the sliders, then drag the scrub bar to feel the result.

Every action, inspected

218 hooks gate each tool call.

Motion constants

Extra scale at the start, eased to 1.0 by the end. 0 = the old flat scrub.

How much of the zoom overscan the camera travels across.

Peak offset of the copy against the world, per stop. 0 = fade only.

Before

  • 560vh track, 5.6 viewports of scroll
  • plain cover-fit, no zoom, no pan
  • copy only faded in and out
  • sample points often pixel-identical

After

  • 360vh track, denser motion per scroll
  • push-in 1.18 to 1.0 plus diagonal drift
  • copy travels about 90px against the world
  • 8 of 8 sample points visibly distinct

Honest limit: this amplifies one short clip. It cannot invent camera moves the source never had. The real fix is generating a scene per stop (intake, benches, tool wall, dock) and seam-joining them, which costs Higgsfield credits. The sliders above make the current asset read as motion; they do not replace shooting more of the world.
